text:
Marie had a dog named Max who could do anything. Max could learn any trick. Every morning, Marie went for a walk, and Max would chase after butterflies. One day, they went for a walk and Max chased after a butterfly that fluttered down a spider web. Instead of catching it, Max climbed onto it. Marie got a fantastic selfie, capturing Max atop the silk wonderland. It was the highlight of her day. Every day.

Then one day, a girl who lived nearby visited her museum. She was there to see Max because she heard stories about him. Max wasn’t interested in butterflies anymore. He had fallen in love. She chased butterflies, but Max caught her eye. Every time he saw her, he’d come running.

Days turned into weeks, and she didn’t go anywhere. Days turned into months. No selfies. No admirers. Just Max.

So Marie wrote an email. In an age where pets had profiles on social media, she uploaded Max’s
